question.content: Read Hp(T) and He(T) from the graph and compare Omega(T)=Delta(T)^2/S(T) where Delta=|Hp-He|, S=Hp+He. Output exactly one expression token sequence in plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: a comparison expression using only the comparison objects and comparison symbols specified in the problem; do not include explanatory text, units, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Read Hp(T) and He(T) from the graph and compare Omega(T)=Delta(T)^2/S(T) where Delta=|Hp-He|, S=Hp+He.
step_1.key_point: The ranking is controlled by the gap Delta and the sum S at each selected temperature.

step_3.content: At T=36.8, He is clearly above Hp, so Delta is nonzero and Omega is larger than near the intersection point.

step_5.content: At T=37.1, Hp and He nearly intersect, so Delta is the smallest and Omega reaches the minimum among the three.

step_7.content: At T=37.5, Hp is much larger while He is low, giving the largest Delta; the squared numerator dominates, so Omega is the largest.
